concerns.text: T020 (Teacher), T021 (Student), and T023 (Training Loop) are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but are semantically dependent. T023 (distill_loop) requires the model definitions from T020/T021 to be instantiated. Furthermore, T022 (Trace Consistency) and T022-INT (Filtering) must precede T023 because the training loop consumes the filtered dataset. Marking these as parallel-safe violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule for model artifacts and data flow.

T015 (Compute entropy/t-test) and T015-FAIL (Fail-fast) are marked [P] but depend on the output of T011-T014 (Generator logic). The generator must produce the data before T015 can compute metrics on it. While T011-T014 might be parallelizable, T015 is strictly sequential to the generation phase. The [P] tag on T015 is incorrect as it implies it can run before data generation completes.

T022 (Compute trace entropy) and T022-INT (Integrate filter) are placed after T020 (Teacher) but before T023 (Training). However, T022 requires the Teacher model (T020) to generate traces. The current ordering implies T022 can run in parallel with T020 (due to [P] on T020) or that T022 is independent. T022 must strictly follow T020. Additionally, T022-INT (filtering) must precede T023 (training) to ensure the training loop receives filtered data, but the [P] tag on T020 suggests T020 and T022 could run concurrently, which is impossible if T022 needs T020's output.

T009 and T010 are marked [P] and placed before T011-T017. While tests can be written in parallel, the 'Independent Test' section of US1 states tests must 'FAIL before implementation'. If T009/T010 are implemented (written) before T011-T017 (implementation), they will fail as expected. However, the [P] tag suggests they can run *execution* in parallel with implementation, which is impossible if they depend on the code being present to fail. Clarify that [P] applies to *authoring* only, not execution, or remove [P] to avoid confusion about execution order.
